food_safe: conditionally (FDA-approved base resin, but FDM layer lines harbor bacteria)
typical_uses: prototyping, decorative prints, cosplay props, low-stress mechanical parts, figurines
pros: easy to print, minimal warping, low odor, biodegradable (industrial composting), wide color range
cons: low heat resistance (~60°C glass transition), brittle under impact, degrades in UV, not suitable for outdoor use
nozzle_notes: any nozzle material works, brass is fine

food_safe: not food safe (FDM layer lines harbor bacteria; base resin is FDA-approved for food contact)
typical_uses: functional parts, water-resistant containers, outdoor signage (short-term), protective covers, mechanical housings
pros: strong layer adhesion, good chemical resistance, low warping compared to ABS, good impact resistance, recyclable
cons: prone to stringing, scratches easily (soft surface), can stick too well to PEI beds (use release agent), hygroscopic
nozzle_notes: brass nozzle is fine, hardened steel for filled variants

typical_uses: functional enclosures, automotive parts, tooling, LEGO-like assemblies, parts needing post-processing (acetone smoothing)
pros: good impact resistance, heat resistant (~100°C), sandable and acetone-smoothable, strong and durable, low moisture absorption
cons: warps significantly without enclosure, emits styrene fumes (ventilation required), high bed temperature needed, prone to layer splitting

typical_uses: phone cases, vibration dampeners, gaskets, wearable devices, wheels, shoe soles, drone bumpers
pros: highly flexible and elastic, excellent impact absorption, good wear and abrasion resistance, chemical resistant
cons: must print slowly (20-30mm/s), difficult retraction (direct drive recommended), stringing, Bowden setups struggle
nozzle_notes: direct drive extruder strongly recommended; Bowden tubes cause buckling. Brass nozzle fine.

typical_uses: gears, hinges, bearings, structural parts, jigs and fixtures, snap-fit assemblies
pros: strongest common FDM material, self-lubricating, excellent fatigue resistance, high impact strength, durable under repeated stress
cons: extremely hygroscopic (must dry before printing and store in dry box), warps significantly, needs enclosure, difficult bed adhesion (use PVA glue)
nozzle_notes: hardened steel nozzle recommended for glass-filled variants; brass fine for unfilled

typical_uses: outdoor enclosures, garden fixtures, automotive exterior parts, signage, weather-exposed functional parts
pros: excellent UV resistance (outdoor use), similar strength to ABS, weather resistant, good surface finish, low moisture absorption
cons: emits styrene fumes (ventilation required), needs enclosure to prevent warping, higher cost than ABS, limited color range from some vendors

typical_uses: safety shields, high-temperature housings, LED light diffusers, engineering prototypes, transparent covers
pros: extremely tough and impact resistant, high heat resistance (~140°C), optically clear prints possible, excellent dimensional stability once printed
cons: very high printing temperatures required, warps aggressively, needs all-metal hotend and enclosure, hygroscopic, can be brittle if not printed correctly
nozzle_notes: all-metal hotend mandatory; PTFE-lined hotends will degrade. Hardened steel nozzle recommended.

typical_uses: water-soluble support material for dual extrusion, complex overhangs, internal cavities
pros: fully water-soluble (dissolves in warm water), leaves clean surface on supported areas, enables geometries impossible with breakaway supports
cons: extremely hygroscopic (absorbs moisture from air within hours), expensive, only useful as support material, requires sealed storage with desiccant, limited shelf life once opened
nozzle_notes: dedicated second extruder recommended; purge thoroughly when switching. Brass nozzle fine.
